---
title: "Recurrent Neural Networks"
subtitle: "Modeling Sequential Data with Memory"
author: "Miguel Fonseca"
format:
  revealjs:
    toc: true
---


# Feedforward Networks vs. Recurrent Networks

## Why Do We Need RNNs?

:::{.columns}
::::{.column width="50%"}
### Feedforward Networks Assume Independence

For MLPs:

$$
\mathbf x_i \perp \mathbf x_j
$$

But many datasets are sequential!

::::

::::{.column width="50%"}
### Recurrent Networks Capture Dependencies


$$
P(\mathbf x_t | \mathbf x_{t-1}, \mathbf x_{t-2}, ..., \mathbf x_1)
$$

Cases where RNNs are useful:

- Text
- Speech
- Financial time-series
- Biological sequences
- Sensor signals
::::
:::

---

## Applications

- Language modeling
- Machine translation
- Speech recognition
- Time-series forecasting
- Genomics
- Video processing

# The Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)

## Core Recurrence

$$
\mathbf z_t =\mathbf W_h \mathbf h_{t-1} + \mathbf W_x \mathbf x_t + \mathbf b
$$

$$
\mathbf h_t = \sigma(\mathbf z_t)
$$

$$
\mathbf y_t = \mathbf W_y \mathbf h_t
$$

Same weights reused across time.

---

## Unrolled Through Time

![Unrolled RNN](/images/rnn_unrolled.svg)

---

## TensorFlow: Simple RNN

```{python}
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow import keras
from tensorflow.keras import layers

model = keras.Sequential([
    layers.SimpleRNN(64, return_sequences=True),
    layers.SimpleRNN(32),
    layers.Dense(1)
])

model.compile(optimizer="adam", loss="mse")
model.summary()
```

---

## Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT)

Training objective:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathscr L}{\partial \mathbf W_h} =\sum_{t=1}^{T}\frac{\partial \mathscr L}{\partial \mathbf h_t}\frac{\partial \mathbf h_t}{\partial \mathbf W_h}
$$

But:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf h_t}{\partial \mathbf h_{t-k}}=\prod_{i=t-k+1}^{t}\frac{\partial \mathbf h_i}{\partial \mathbf h_{i-1}}
$$

with:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf h_i}{\partial \mathbf h_{i-1}}
=\mathbf W_h \cdot \text{diag}(\sigma'(\mathbf z_t))
$$

Repeated matrix multiplication → instability.

## The Vanishing Gradient Problem

Simplified Recurrence

$$
\mathbf h_t = \sigma(\mathbf W_h \mathbf h_{t-1})
$$

Derivative:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf h_t}{\partial \mathbf h_{t-1}}
=
\mathbf W_h \cdot \text{diag}(\sigma'(\mathbf z))
$$

Repeated products:

$$
\prod_{i=1}^{k} \mathbf W_h \boldsymbol{\lambda}_i
$$


:::{.columns}

::::{.column width="50%"}
If:

$$
\rho(\mathbf W_h) < 1
$$

>Gradient vanishes.

::::

::::{.column width="50%"}

If:

$$
\rho(\mathbf W_h) > 1
$$

>Exploding gradient.

::::

:::

# Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)

## LSTM Gate Equations

:::{.callout-note}
Allow constant error flow through a memory cell.
:::

**Forget gate:**

$$
\mathbf f_t = \sigma\left(\mathbf W_f [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_f\right)
$$

**Input gate:**

$$
\mathbf i_t = \sigma\left(\mathbf W_i [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_i\right)
$$


**Output:**

$$
\mathbf o_t = \sigma\left(\mathbf W_o [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_o\right)
$$

**Input Node**

$$
\widetilde{\mathbf c} = \tanh\left(\mathbf W_c [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_c\right)
$$

---

![LSTM Cell Update with Input](/images/lstm-1.svg){width=70%}

---

## Cell update

$$
\mathbf c_t = \mathbf f_t \odot \mathbf c_{t-1} + \mathbf i_t \odot \widetilde{\mathbf c}_t
$$

$$
\mathbf h_t = \mathbf o_t \odot \tanh(\mathbf c_t)
$$

---

![LSTM cell](/images/lstm-3.svg)

---

## Why LSTM Works

If:

$$
\mathbf f_t \approx 1
$$

Then:

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf c_t}{\partial \mathbf c_{t-1}} \approx 1
$$

>Gradient preserved.

---

## Tensorflow: LSTM

```{python}
model = keras.Sequential([
    layers.LSTM(64, return_sequences=True),
    layers.LSTM(32),
    layers.Dense(1)
])

model.compile(optimizer="adam", loss="mse")
```

# Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)

## GRU Equations

>Simpler alternative to LSTM.

**Update gate:**

$$
\mathbf z_t = \sigma\left(\mathbf W_z [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_z\right)
$$

**Reset gate:**

$$
\mathbf r_t = \sigma\left(\mathbf W_r [\mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_r\right)
$$

**Candidate cell state:**

$$
\widetilde{\mathbf h}_t = \tanh\left(\mathbf W_h [\mathbf r_t \! \odot \! \mathbf h_{t-1} \, \mathbf x_t] + \mathbf b_h\right)
$$

---

![GRU Cell Update](/images/gru-2.svg)

---

## GRU Update

$$
\mathbf h_t = (1 - \mathbf z_t) \odot \mathbf h_{t-1} + \mathbf z_t \odot \widetilde{\mathbf h}_t
$$

---

![GRU Cell](/images/gru-3.svg)
---

## Tensorflow: GRU

```{python}
model = keras.Sequential([
    layers.GRU(64, return_sequences=True),
    layers.GRU(32),
    layers.Dense(1)
])
```

# Regularization in RNNs

## Recurrent Dropout

Standard dropout disrupts temporal memory.

**Recurrent dropout:**

- Applied on recurrent connections
- Same mask across timesteps

---

## TensorFlow: Recurrent Dropout

```{python}
model = keras.Sequential([
    layers.LSTM(
        64,
        return_sequences=True,
        dropout=0.2,
        recurrent_dropout=0.2
    ),
    layers.LSTM(32),
    layers.Dense(1)
])
```

---

## Gradient Clipping

$$
\nabla_{\boldsymbol \theta} \mathscr L \leftarrow \frac{\nabla_{\boldsymbol \theta} \mathscr L}{\max(1, \frac{||\nabla_{\boldsymbol \theta} \mathscr L||}{\lambda})}
$$

Prevent exploding gradients:

```{python}
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(
    learning_rate=0.001,
    clipnorm=1.0
)
```

# Extensions

## Deep RNNs

Stack multiple RNN layers for hierarchical representations:

![Deep RNN](/images/rnn_deep.svg)

---

## Bidirectional RNNs

Process sequences in both directions:

![Bidirectional RNN](/images/rnn_bidirectional.svg)

# Final Notes

## Model Comparison

| Model | Long Dependencies | Complexity | Speed  |
| ----- | ----------------- | ---------- | ------ |
| RNN   | Poor              | Low        | Fast   |
| LSTM  | Excellent         | High       | Slower |
| GRU   | Very Good         | Medium     | Faster |

## Limitations of RNNs

- Sequential computation
- Hard to parallelize
- Long sequences still challenging
- Transformers dominate NLP
